leadText: The Snowball (Japanese: ゆきだま Snowball) is an item introduced in Generation VI.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 Held item 1.2 Bag item 1.3 Other uses Effect [ edit | edit source ] Held item [ edit | edit source ] If the holder is hit by a Ice-type damaging move , it consumes the Snowball and its Attack is increased by one stage. It does not activate if the holder already has +6 Attack stat stages (unless its Ability is Contrary , in which case it will not activate if the holder has -6 Attack stat stages). It does not activate if the holder is protected from or unaffected by the Ice-type move (e.g. due to Telepathy or Wonder Guard ). If multiple Pokémon each holding a Snowball are hit by an Ice-type move at once, each hit Pokémon will consume its Snowball before Symbiosis transfers any item (including Snowballs). Bag item [ edit | edit source ] In Pokémon Legends: Arceus , if the player has Snowballs in their satchel , they can be thrown at wild Pokémon to temporarily stun them. Other uses [ edit | edit source ] In Pokémon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire , if a Snowball is held by a nicknamed Regice in the party, along with Regirock and Registeel , it allows Regigigas to spawn in Island Cave , except at night or before defeating or catching Groudon OR / Kyogre AS .

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Held item Games Description X Y OR AS S M US UM An item to be held by a Pokémon. It boosts Attack if hit with an Ice-type attack. It can only be used once. P E Sw Sh BD SP LA An item to be held by a Pokémon. It boosts Attack if the holder is hit with an Ice-type attack. It can only be used once. S V An item to be held by a Pokémon. This single-use ball of snow boosts the Attack stat if the holder is damaged by an Ice-type move. Hisui Games Description LA S V A hard ball made of compressed snow. Hitting a Pokémon with one may stun the Pokémon, creating an opening for you to act.

Acquisition [ edit | edit source ] Games Finite methods Repeatable methods X Y Snow piles OR AS Battle Maison (32 BP ) Held by wild Snorunt (5% chance) S M US UM Held by wild Vulpix and wild Snorunt (5% chance each) Sw Sh Circhester Battle Tower (10 BP ) Held by wild Snorunt and wild Snom (5% chance each) Sw Sh IA Cram-o-matic ( Ice : 2-20 points, 82-90 points) Sw Sh CT Hidden recurring item ( Slippery Slope , Frostpoint Field , Snowslide Slope ) LA Snow piles ( Alabaster Icelands ) S V Delibird Presents ( Cascarrafa Branch, after earning 4 Gym Badges ) Sparkling overworld item ( Glaseado Mountain ) S V ID Sparkling overworld item ( Polar Biome ) Distribution [ edit | edit source ] Games Event Language/Region Distribution period S M Pokémon Center Alolan Vulpix items Japan February 13 to May 13, 2017
